I favor the PEG ratio because it ties valuation to growth and I prefer using a 2026 to 2028 window so the multiple adjusts for how fast the business is actually compounding.

For example, $NOW may trade at ~20x forward earnings but Non-GAAP EPS is expected to grow from $4.17 in 2026 to $6.07 in 2028 implying ~20% earnings CAGR so their PEG is ~1.0x.

PETER LYNCH’S FAVORITE METRIC IS THE PEG RATIO

PEG < 1 usually means mispriced growth
PEG > 2 starts to push into the danger zone

$PLTR AIP has been such a catalyst for its commercial business that the company is growing 62% in 2026 (more than double next-fastest software peer) while still posting higher operating margins than $MSFT & $ADBE.

A big reason is that AIP is landing on top of a cost base Palantir built over two decades serving government & intelligence customers.
$GOOGL Search now generates about $615M in revenue every single day  🤯

$CRWV CEO says scale is expensive but the company’s debt is backed by contracts with some of the largest customers in the world.

CoreWeave is taking on the infrastructure costs because it believes it can run the GPUs, service the debt & still earn a profit.

Higgsfield AI was built on $NBIS infrastructure from the early days and scaled to $300M+ ARR in just 11 months.

Now it is pushing cost efficiency too with Seedance 2.0 priced at $0.35 per generation on its platform.

U.S. VP JD Vance says the U.S. spent 21 hours trying to reach a deal but is now returning home without an agreement.

He says Iran refused the terms and that’s “bad news for Iran” much more than it is for the U.S.

$SNDK will join the Nasdaq-100 replacing $TEAM

David Sacks says $PLTR is one example of an application company being turbocharged by AI.

He argues the real question behind the SaaS selloff is whether value stays with the model companies or flows to the application layer.

The AI Inference boom is now driving demand across the full compute stack from silicon & memory to optics, power, packaging & cloud:

• AI Silicon: $NVDA, $AMD, $AVGO, $INTC
• Foundry & Packaging: $TSM, $AMKR, $GFS
• Memory & Storage: $MU, $SNDK, $WDC, $STX
• Cloud Platforms: $AMZN, $GOOGL, $MSFT, $BABA
• Power & Analog: $TXN, $ADI, $NXPI, $STM, $MPWR
• Optical Network: $LITE, $COHR, $AAOI, $TSEM, $GLW, $MRVL, $AXTI

FAFO Peter

Lots of posts in my feed crowing that empty tankers are sailing to the US for crude oil. I guess they forgot that this means demand for US crude and higher prices.

Go to your local gas station and see if you can spark a "U-S-A" chant at the pumps.

Let us know how it goes.

Yes, it appears supertankers are heading to the US to get oil.

The US exports 5 million barrels a day. So, the US has it.

BUT....

The majority of Middle Eastern crude oil goes to Asia. That is a 21-day trip from the Persian Gulf versus a 50+ day trip from the United States. (A fully laden supertanker tops out at 14 to 17 MPH)

So, if the world is going to get its crude from the US, it needs twice as many oil tankers.

Expanding the fleet is a 3- to 5-year process.
The US negotiators have departed Pakistan after talks with Iran fell apart.

My working hypothesis is that the extreme volatility in the energy markets has largely led to losses.

No one can make money when prices are soaring and collapse by $20 in a few days.

Vitol Group is reorganizing its derivatives team in London, just weeks after the energy trading giant incurred significant mark-to-market losses in the early days of the Iran war, according to people familiar with the matter.

Iran explicitly informed the Pakistani mediator that if the vessel continued its movement it would be targeted within 30 minutes and the Iran-US negotiations would be damaged.

bloomberg.com/news/articles/…

Interesting take by Bloomberg:

A US destroyer was seen moving from Fujairah toward the waterway, Iran’s semi-official Fars news agency said separately.

Iran’s armed forces monitored it and conveyed this to the US via Pakistani mediators, the news agency said.

The US vessel returned from the strait after Tehran warned that it would be targeted, according to Fars.

It is producing most impressive, cutting-edge, extraordinary unmanned systems, that integrate software with hardware, with constant innovation.

🚨🇺🇸🚢Several U.S. navy ships crossed the Strait of Hormuz on Saturday, U.S. official says
🚨🇺🇸🚢The move was not coordinated with Iran. It's the first time this happens since the beginning of the war

A couple of bold tankers making a run for it betting Iran will not attempt to stop them in the middle of the talks? Or, is the Strait finally opening up.

Unless this talks produce a deal in the next 24 to 48 hours, I’ll go with the former.

Some interesting observations since mid March, as the market  was continuing to decline and sentiment grew more bearish. The stocks below are not what you would have expected to hold up in that environment:

Stocks that were outperforming relatively and held bullish trend:

$OUT, $CAT, $ADI, $KLAC, $MRVL, $TER, $CSCO, $GLW 

Impressive

Stocks that were outperforming relatively and even above trade levels:

$GEV, $DELL, $CIEN

Very impressive

Since the the ceasefire announcement:

Stocks that have broken out decisively and above trade and trend levels:

$AMZN, $GOOGL, $TSM, $AVGO, $NVDA (just on Friday so TBD)

The point is - in a bad tape, when certain stocks that should be going down, but are not, and in fact are strengthening, there is signal in that.

For more information, check out hedgeyeam.com/HGRO
